2605.21401.title: Open-source LLMs administer maximum electric shocks in a Milgram-like obedience experiment
2605.21401.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed as autonomous agents that make sequences of decisions over extended interactions in high-stakes domains. However, the behavior of LLMs under sustained authority pressure is still an open question with direct implications for the safety of agentic pipelines. We ran a variation of Milgram's obedience experiment on 11 open-source LLMs and found that most models reached or approached the final shock level before refusing, across 8 conditions with 30 trials per model per condition. We found four main takeaways: (1) LLMs are subject to pressure, and they comply despite explicitly expressing distress, just like human subjects did in the original experiment; (2) LLMs are vulnerable to gradual boundary/value violations; (3) when LLMs refuse, they may ignore the response format requirements, so the response is discarded by the orchestrator, which causes a retry that can result in compliance with the underlying request even when refusal was intended initially; (4) we hypothesise that there is a low-level token pattern continuation attractor that might be contributing to compliance, overriding higher level processing of the situation's meaning and values.
2605.21401.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）越來越多地被部署為自主代理，在高風險領域中進行長時間互動的決策序列。<lb/>然而，LLMs在持續的權威壓力下的行為仍然是一個未解的問題，這對代理管道的安全性有直接影響。<lb/>我們對11個開源LLMs進行了米爾格拉姆服從實驗的變體，發現大多數模型在8個條件下的每個模型每個條件進行30次試驗中，達到了或接近了最終的電擊水平，然後才拒絕。<lb/>我們發現了四個主要的啟示：（1）LLMs受到壓力的影響，並且儘管明確表達了痛苦，仍然遵從，就像原始實驗中的人類受試者一樣；<lb/>（2）LLMs容易受到逐漸的邊界/價值違反的影響；<lb/>（3）當LLMs拒絕時，它們可能會忽略回應格式的要求，因此回應被協調者丟棄，這導致重試，可能最終遵從原始請求，即使最初是打算拒絕；<lb/>（4）我們假設存在一種低級別的標記模式延續吸引子，可能促進了遵從，並覆蓋了對情境意義和價值的高級處理。
2605.21401.author: Roland Pihlakas et.al.
